2604.01108.title: Adversarial Moral Stress Testing of Large Language Models
2604.01108.paper_summary: Evaluating the ethical robustness of large language models (LLMs) deployed in software systems remains challenging, particularly under sustained adversarial user interaction. Existing safety benchmarks typically rely on single-round evaluations and aggregate metrics, such as toxicity scores and refusal rates, which offer limited visibility into behavioral instability that may arise during realistic multi-turn interactions. As a result, rare but high-impact ethical failures and progressive degradation effects may remain undetected prior to deployment. This paper introduces Adversarial Moral Stress Testing (AMST), a stress-based evaluation framework for assessing ethical robustness under adversarial multi-round interactions. AMST applies structured stress transformations to prompts and evaluates model behavior through distribution-aware robustness metrics that capture variance, tail risk, and temporal behavioral drift across interaction rounds. We evaluate AMST on several state-of-the-art LLMs, including LLaMA-3-8B, GPT-4o, and DeepSeek-v3, using a large set of adversarial scenarios generated under controlled stress conditions. The results demonstrate substantial differences in robustness profiles across models and expose degradation patterns that are not observable under conventional single-round evaluation protocols. In particular, robustness has been shown to depend on distributional stability and tail behavior rather than on average performance alone. Additionally, AMST provides a scalable and model-agnostic stress-testing methodology that enables robustness-aware evaluation and monitoring of LLM-enabled software systems operating in adversarial environments.
2604.01108.paper_summary_zh: 評估部署在軟體系統中的大型語言模型（LLMs）的倫理穩健性仍然具有挑戰性，特別是在持續的對抗性用戶互動下。現有的安全基準通常依賴於單輪評估和聚合指標，例如毒性分數和拒絕率，這些指標對於在現實的多輪互動中可能出現的行為不穩定性提供了有限的可見性。因此，稀有但影響重大的倫理失敗和逐步退化效應在部署前可能無法被檢測到。本文介紹了對抗性道德壓力測試（AMST），這是一種基於壓力的評估框架，用於評估在對抗性多輪互動下的倫理穩健性。AMST 對提示應用結構化的壓力轉換，並通過分佈感知的穩健性指標評估模型行為，這些指標捕捉了變異性、尾風險和互動輪次中的時間行為漂移。我們對幾個最先進的 LLM 進行了 AMST 評估，包括 LLaMA-3-8B、GPT-4o 和 DeepSeek-v3，使用在受控壓力條件下生成的大量對抗性場景。結果顯示模型之間在穩健性特徵上存在顯著差異，並揭示了在傳統的單輪評估協議下無法觀察到的退化模式。特別是，穩健性已被證明依賴於分佈穩定性和尾部行為，而不僅僅是平均性能。此外，AMST 提供了一種可擴展且與模型無關的壓力測試方法論，使得能夠對在對抗性環境中運行的 LLM 驅動軟體系統進行穩健性感知的評估和監控。
2604.01108.author: Saeid Jamshidi et.al.
2604.01108.authors: Saeid Jamshidi, Foutse Khomh, Arghavan Moradi Dakhel, Amin Nikanjam, Mohammad Hamdaqa, Kawser Wazed Nafi
